**Clinical Reasoning:**
Radiopaque areas near the root apex of young permanent teeth affected by chronic pulpitis indicate a specific type of reaction characterized by chronic inflammation rather than active infection, consistent with **chronic apical periodontitis**. Given the description aligns perfectly with option B — *Apical condensing osteitis* involves new bone formation around the root tip under conditions of chronic inflammation and edema, matching our scenario precisely.
